---
title: "Veteran Lung Cancer Survival Analysis"
format: html
---

# 👋 Welcome

This site showcases a survival analysis project based on a Veterans Administration clinical trial investigating treatment outcomes in patients with advanced lung cancer.

It includes **two versions** of the same core analysis — one for public-facing presentation, and one built as a **teaching notebook and personal reference**.

---

## 📘 Teaching Notebook

> _“Teach it to remember it.”_

This version of the report includes **collapsible callout sections** explaining:

- The theory behind each step (e.g., proportional hazards, residuals)
- Practical notes on survival analysis in R
- Code snippets and diagnostics used in the modeling process

The goal is to create a **reusable reference** — something I can revisit when refreshing my skills, and something that reflects a deep understanding of the methods.

👉 [Open Teaching Notebook »](notebooks/veteran-lung-cancer-coxph.html)

---

## 📄 Final Report

This is a **clean, styled version of the report** — stripped of callouts and teaching notes — suitable for:

- Demonstrating applied survival analysis skills
- Portfolio inclusion

---

## 🛠️ About This Project

Built in R using `survival`, `survminer`, and `ggplot2`, this project walks through:

- Exploratory Kaplan-Meier analysis
- Cox proportional hazards modeling
- Model diagnostics and residuals
- Spline transformations and interaction effects

All rendered using **Quarto** for seamless publishing and reproducibility.

---

_TL;DR: One notebook teaches, one notebook impresses._
